page: Amazon Web Services g4dn.16xlarge is a graphics-intensive, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores based on an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, the instance is equipped with a single NVIDIA T4 GPU from the Turing family, which provides 16 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by strong memory and GPU-accelerated capabilities alongside weaker single-core CPU metrics. Single-core performance is positioned in the bottom 25 percent, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 1285.28 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1104.0. Conversely, multi-core and memory performance are robust, with large-block memory read bandwidth reaching 210.38 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 93.59 GB/sec, both in the top 25 percent. The instance excels in LLM inference tasks; prompt processing for small (135M) and medium (7B) models ranks in the top 10 percent, achieving 8259.78 and 1162.72 tokens per second respectively, while text generation for a 7B model also reaches the top 10 percent at 41.95 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the g4dn.16xlarge offers a balanced resource density for workloads that can leverage both high-speed local NVMe storage and GPU acceleration without requiring top-tier single-threaded CPU performance. The combination of 50 Gbps network bandwidth, dedicated vCPUs, and a dedicated GPU makes it highly suitable for machine learning inference, small-to-medium LLM deployments, graphics-intensive applications, and database operations. It is less optimal for single-threaded CPU-bound tasks, where its lower single-core benchmark scores represent a resource-to-performance tradeoff.
description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.16xlarge is a graphics-intensive, network-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory, a 900 GB NVMe SSD, and a single NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network speed of 50 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate a tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is weak, falling in the bottom 25 percent, whereas large-block memory bandwidth and LLM inference speeds are strong to top-tier, with medium 7B model prompt processing reaching 1162.72 tokens per second. This profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for GPU-accelerated workloads, machine learning inference, and graphics rendering, while remaining less suited for single-threaded CPU tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.16xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, a 900 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA T4 GPU, optimized for machine learning inference and graphics-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, a 900 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA T4 GPU for accelerated machine learning and graphics.
tagline: An accelerated x86_64 platform combining dedicated Intel Xeon processors, local NVMe storage, and an NVIDIA T4 GPU for efficient machine learning inference.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256 GB DDR4 RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL CPU
Dedicated NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB VRAM for graphics and ML acceleration
900 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier LLM inference performance for small and medium-sized models
Weaker single-core CPU performance offset by strong multi-core capabilities
Best suited for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and database operations
